description_long: This is a small, ornate pagoda structure with two prominent, green-tiled, tiered roofs and a white-walled body. The lower level features a wraparound porch with white railings and several windows, while the upper level is more compact and also encircled by a balcony. Hanging lanterns are suspended from the corners of the upper roof, adding decorative detail. The roof tiles have a distinct, dark green coloration with subtle striping, and the eaves are slightly flared but not dramatically upturned. The entrance is marked by a set of steps leading to a wooden door, and the overall design is symmetrical and compact. The structure appears to be made primarily of wood and tile, with some metal or stone elements for the roof cap and lanterns.
description: A compact, two-tiered pagoda with green-tiled roofs, white walls, wraparound porches, and hanging lanterns. The design is symmetrical and features a wooden entrance door and decorative roof details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, steps, and symmetrical arrangement of windows and lanterns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered roof structure, balcony railings, and lanterns hanging from the upper roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a wooden door on the upper level, balcony, and the full width of the lower roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the porch, windows, and the stacked roof design.
